Video.summary: David Blanton heads North to hunt turkeys with new friend Evan Putnam. David gets the true Maine experience catching lobster one evening and following up with an amazing turkey hunt.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "David Maine"

Video.summary: This episode we make our way through Georgia, where we stop in Helen, Alpharetta and Atlanta!!
Video.title: "We Crushed Them! Fishing the HEART of Georgia (Atlanta)"

Image.alt: "We Crushed Them! Fishing the HEART of Georgia (Atlanta)"
"We Crushed Them! Fishing the HEART of Georgia (Atlanta)"
"We Crushed Them! Fishing the HEART of Georgia (Atlanta)"

Video.summary: This episode we make our way through South Carolina, where we stop in Clemson, Travelers Rest and Charleston!!
Video.title: "Lowcountry Buzzer Beater! (Redfish on Fly)"

Video.summary: Meet Ryan, a seasoned St. Petersburg guide, fighting tides, time, and the last great tarpon run of the season.
Video.title: "Ryan Harrington"

Video.summary: Meet Captain Lionel James — a respected Destin, Florida guide known for his upbeat personality, strong community ties, and love for fitness when he’s not on the water.
Video.title: "Lionel James"

Video.summary: In part 1 of the British episodes, Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the beautiful venison that Michelin star chef, Mark Robinson has to offer. He then proceeds to travel to the border of Scotland where he samples some of the finest and most historic bird shooting practices found throughout the British Isles.
Video.title: Wildbound - "UK Part 1"

Video.summary: Establishing themselves just after the last Ice Age, the blacktail of Vancouver Island offers an important genetic population isolated from the mainland. To explore the local relationship with the species, its harvesting, conservation and history, Dan Cabela travels to the island.
Video.title: "The Blacktail of Vancouver Island"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair heads up to Steinhatchee after getting a call from local tournament angler Jay Andrew. The reds have been popping off and so the two head out in search.
Video.title: "Top Water Reds with Jay Andrew"

Video.summary: The second day in Steinhatchee, Capt. Blair & Jay finally find where all the reds got off too, and I mean ALL the reds. This is one of the craziest sights on the show yet.
Video.title: "The School"

Video.summary: Highlights from Capt. Blair's recent travels.
Video.title: "Bits & Bites"

Video.summary: We follow along as a decommissioned ship is sunk to create a new artificial reef, much to the delight of divers and anglers. Plus, Adapt-Able SCUBA makes underwater adventure possible for those who didn’t - think it was.
Video.title: "Reef Dropping and Diving For All"

Video.summary: When Justin and Meredith stay positive after a series of bad breaks, it pays off in fun. If fun means fish. After all, we are in Panama City Beach.
Video.title: "Positive = Good Day"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor meet up with Captain Nick LaBadie at Big Pine Key for an action packed day on the flats chasing tarpon, bonefish and permit for the elusive grand slam.
Video.title: "Double Slam with Captain Nick LaBadie"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor run from Hawks Cay Resort up to Islamorada on a windy day to fish under the mangroves for Goliath grouper.
Video.title: "Goliaths Under the Bushes"

Video.summary: We highlight organizations and programs making a difference in the Florida Keys. Learn how they help protect and restore these vital ecosystems.
Video.title: "Groups working to restore our vital ecosystems"

Image.alt: "Groups working to restore our vital ecosystems"
"Groups working to restore our vital ecosystems"
"Groups working to restore our vital ecosystems"

Video.summary: This episode we make our way through Florida, where we stop in Jacksonville, Jupiter, Boca Raton and Miami!!
Video.title: "We Spent 14 Days Fly Fishing Florida"

Video.summary: This episode we make our way through Florida, where we stop in Key West, Marathon, Bonita Springs, and Sanibel Island.
Video.title: "Pursuing the Fabled Grand Slam (Florida PT 2)"

Video.summary: An epic Gould’s turkey hunt in Mexico features high-country gobbles, close-range encounters, expert calling, and unforgettable moments on this incredible adventure.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Mexico Goulds"

Video.summary: Slade and the old Trained Assassins crew are headed back to Montrose in pursuit of a big elk. The conditions are not favorable but the crew is sticking it out!
Video.title: "Montrose Elk Pt. 1"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade goes over what he’s doing at his home property to create summer food plots for deer, and gives tips to those looking to do the same. - On the final hunt for the 2024 season, Lori gets a big Wilkinson County gobbler.
Video.title: "Summer Plots"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade and his brother Tai take us around a new investment property - a 55 acre tract in Wilkinson County, MS and go over what it took to take an undesirable raw tract, to a place with multiple offers on the first day it was put on the market.
Video.title: "Double Nickel"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade shows us around some of his investment properties, and Ryan kills a nice bull elk in New Mexico.
Video.title: "Winding Road"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade talks about some of the things he does to get - ready for the October 1st bow season opener in Mississippi, and we - show a couple doe hunts from the - first couple days of the season.
Video.title: "Doe Days"

Video.summary: He’s the original fishing coach. From the helm of one of the most iconic inshore shows ever made, Captain C.A. Richardson has taught a generation how to fish. This documentary dives into his story. The man, the mission, and the waters that made him who he is.
Video.title: "CA"

Video.summary: LBP Spotlights Internet Personality and YouTube Star Lawson Lindsey as he searches for trophy fish at the Headwaters of the Loxahatchee River.
Video.title: "Lawson Lindsey"

Video.summary: Denise Sanchez was awarded a hunt at G2 following our Hunters Extravaganza drawings. Its great to get to spend time on the ranch with new members of TTHA. Denise gets familiar with a giant and learns about new technology while on this hunt.
Video.title: "A Newfound Passion"

Video.summary: We find ourselves fishing with Troy from Lonestar Beer. Not only is Troy a great friend he is also a big supporter of Hunters Extravaganza. We get to catch up in the offseason and spend a day on the water discussing future events and reflect on what all Texas has to offer.
Video.title: "Lonestar Redfish"

Video.summary: Cable is a huge supporter of Texas Hunting and everything Teas Trophy Hunters. We are blessed to spend a few days with hum at the beautiful Saddle Creek Ranch in Brady Texas. Greg has a tight management plan and we are glad we got to enjoy his ranch with him and his team.
Video.title: "Cable Smith"

Video.summary: WE join Bobby Falkenberg as he joins Bret Ferguson from Double T Outfitters. Bobby is an avid outdoorsman, judge, and father of 5. Bret manages 30,000 acres in Ozona TX, the knowledge he has of every inch of this amazing low fence ranch is remarkable. Bobby has an action packed 2 day hunt.
Video.title: "Double T Outfitters"

Video.summary: While up in Alaska, the crew caught more fish than we had time to show so here's another look at some of the fish that were caught on the Alaskan trip.
Video.title: "Alaska Bits & Bites"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff dive into the wild waters off Hawaii’s coast, coming face-to-face with sharks and graceful manta rays. The adrenaline peaks when Kyle takes a heart-pounding leap off a towering 40-foot cliff, making this adventure as daring as it is unforgettable.
Video.title: "Fishing Hawaii"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff explore the breathtaking Waipio Valley, the Hawaiian "Valley of the Kings," in search of prawns and bullfrogs. Their adventure continues up a massive volcano, hunting invasive rams, before ending with a feast of native fish paired with island fruits and spices.
Video.title: "Hunting Hawaii"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff hear about the dangers of fishing for Bluefin Tuna in the Atlantic Ocean from locals in Gloucester, Massachusetts. Then they head 30 miles out onto the open ocean with Darrin of Reel Respect Charters in the hopes of landing their own giant.
Video.title: "Gloucester Giant Bluefin"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff team up with their friends from Boss Shotshells in South Dakota for a high-energy upland hunt, pursuing wild pheasants with the help of skilled hunting dogs. The adventure takes a quirky turn with a visit to the iconic Corn Palace.
Video.title: "South Dakota Pheasant"

Video.summary: Michael Pitts and Anthony Virga join Realtree Spring Thunder producer Matt Garger to turkey hunt on his home turf in New York's Adirondack Mountains.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "New York Turkey Hunting"

Video.summary: 2 down, 1 to go. Anthony Virga’s the next man up for a gobbler with Michael Pitts and Matt Garger in New York’s Adirondack Mountains.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Virga New York Kill"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin Leake and a fellow guide search for redfish along the beach, but Mother Nature has other ideas and gives them a show they didn’t expect.
Video.title: "Red to Black to Jack"

Video.summary: For Capt. Justin Leake, sight fishing the beach is one of the most productive, and FUN ways to fish. When that doesn’t work, Capt. Todd Jones suggests fishing an artificial reef, and as it turns out, that’s where dinner lives.
Video.title: "Beach For Fun Reef For Flavor"

Video.summary: Meredith McCord arrives in late spring and joins Capt. Justin Leake on the hunt for a fish she’s never caught before. Lo and behold, it’s a day fit for a king.
Video.title: "First Things First"

Video.summary: Famous for its Mayan ruins, the Yucatan in Mexico is home to a diverse array of wildlife. After years of over-harvesting and exploitation, regulated hunting is helping to conserve large areas of this fragile ecosystem. Dan Cabela travels to the region to find out more.
Video.title: "Into the Yucatán"

Video.summary: South Africa offers an incredible insight into the hunter and gatherer history of ancestors. Dan Cabela re-traces the footsteps of humanity's forefathers and explores how modern, private ownership of land has helped re-balance the human relationship with nature.
Video.title: "The hunting heritage of South Africa"

Video.summary: This episode is all about thanking our service men and women who have served or are still currently serving; Our pals Wes Thomas and Terry McWilliams join in to help thank these heroes, along with great companies like IN 811 and Centerpoint; special thanks to West Bogg Park for having us again this year.
Video.title: "Tribute to Heroes"

Video.summary: This week we join professional angler, Andrew Nordbye, for a cold day on Guntersville to catch some giant crappie; traveling through a major winter storm and arriving at an abandoned lake was the not-surprising scene for the day. Andrew's priorities are in order when it comes to his marriage, his faith, and his family, including just-arrived new babies...but he sill made time to squeeze in this day on water with us!
Video.title: "Alabama Artic Crappies"

Video.summary: The sharks won’t stop Capt. Jared and special Guest Capt. Jimmy Hendricks from having their day.
Video.title: "Variety Pack"

Video.summary: Joined by PCB’s Artificial Reef Coordinator Erin Graham Capt Justin - fish previously dropped reefs before sinking the famed BLACK PEARL from iconic films right off Panama City Beach, FL.
Video.title: "Black Pearl’s Final Resting Place"

Video.summary: Kyle and Jeff embark on a 20-day mission into the Everglades to find the invasive pythons that are plaguing the ecosystem. These apex predators have eliminated more than 90% of fur-bearing animals in their new home. Can they turn the tide, or is the Everglades already lost?
Video.title: "The Everglades Python"

Video.summary: On this episode of Hunting Land Man, Slade does more summer food plot prep in hopes "Pope" will make an appearance! With Rain on the way Slade is able to disk and seed his "Pope" Summer spot!
Video.title: "The Plan for "POPE"!"

Video.summary: The "Trained Assassins" are back and it’s turkey season in the Midwest. In this first episode, Slade, Blake, and Ryan start in Missouri, run into some trouble, but end up getting it done on the first leg of the 2024 turkey tour.
Video.title: "Midwest Turkey Tour 2024"

Video.summary: In the 1990s, commercial clamming decimated the Indian River Lagoon clam population. Devoid of clams—which naturally filter the water—the lagoon’s water quality rapidly deteriorated as algae blooms went unchecked and sunlight could no longer reach the seagrass that is so vital to the lagoon’s ecosystem. 30 years later, a professor at the University of Florida teams up with a well-known deep sea fishing guide to launch the Indian River Clam Restoration Project. After scouring the lagoon and finding ONLY 39 surviving clams, Dr. Todd Osborne and Captain Blair Wiggins—with the help of local residents and businesses—begin carefully breeding “super clams" to restore the dwindling clam population in the Indian River Lagoon. “The Captain & the Super Clams” is more than a human intervention success story; it’s a beautiful buddy film that shows the power of community, science, and technology coming together to find an innovative solution for bio-diversity protection.
Video.title: "The Captain & the Super Clams"

Video.summary: “Part of our responsibility as citizens in this country is to make sure we not only take care of these resources but we hand it off to the next generation in better condition that we found it.” -Bob Ziehmer, Senior Director of Conservation for Bass Pro Shops & Cabela's Join Bob in New Mexico as he talks about taking care of our natural resources for future generations. Special thanks to Express UU Bar Ranch
Video.title: RMEF - "Better Than We Found It"

Video.summary: Brandon explores the sunken vessels and beautiful waters of the Florida Panhandle with local guide, Brady Hale.
Video.title: "Diving the Artificial Reefs of Pensacola"

Video.summary: Misty and the Live Wildly team head south to the Florida Keys diving into conservation on the underwater reef’s, chasing down Lobsters and clean up in unusual place.
Video.title: "How to Live Wildly: Connecting you with Nature"

Image.alt: "How to Live Wildly: Connecting you with Nature"
"How to Live Wildly: Connecting you with Nature"
"How to Live Wildly: Connecting you with Nature"

Video.summary: From time to time you have moments that will change the way think about something. That is what happened for us while hunting the Scimitar Oryx with Republic Outdoors. Hunting is more than harvesting animals, it’s conservation, and in this story, we learn how a species nearly extinct gets another chance to live.
Video.title: "A Story Of Restoration"

Video.summary: In 2010, Amberly Snyder’s life changed forever when an accident left her paralyzed from the waist-down. Her comeback story not unlike something you’d see in a movie…in fact it inspired one. Disabled Outdoorsmen USA contacted TTHA and set up this hunt and we were fortunate enough to be able to capture just some of Amberly’s story and her harvest of a beautiful Axis buck at the G2 Ranch in the spring of 2022.
Video.title: "Walk, Ride, Rodeo…Hunt"

Video.summary: The bond between and parent and child can grow in many ways, but nothing compares to the bond shared when hunting. The annual Father-Son hunt at FE Hill Outfitters has becoming a tradition for Heath and his son Gavin and in 2021, Gavin was able to take his largest buck to date, with his dad Heath behind the camera.
Video.title: "Father - Son Tradition"

Video.summary: Follow TTHA President/CEO Christina Pittman and her husband Matt this week as they tag along side Dr. James Kroll for his annual deer day! This is a great opportunity to learn from one of the best. We are blessed to be able to share his knowledge with other like minded outdoor enthusiasts.
Video.title: "Deer Day"

Video.summary: The crew makes it to Oklahoma. Unfortunately, the weather is not in their favor, but that doesn't slow them down. The morning breaks light, and the crew hears dozens of gobbles.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "7-Day Slam: Windy Conditions and Screaming Oklahoma Gobblers | Spring Thunder"

Image.alt: Spring Thunder - "7-Day Slam: Windy Conditions and Screaming Oklahoma Gobblers | Spring Thunder"
Spring Thunder - "7-Day Slam: Windy Conditions and Screaming Oklahoma Gobblers | Spring Thunder"
Spring Thunder - "7-Day Slam: Windy Conditions and Screaming Oklahoma Gobblers | Spring Thunder"

Video.summary: It's the next Grand Slam stop. The Spring Thunder crew hunts eastern wild turkeys in Georgia. They get there just in the nick of time. When they arrive at producer James Thompsons' farm, a screaming bird is waiting for them.
Video.title: "7-Day Slam: Eastern Turkeys in the Swamp | "

Image.alt: "7-Day Slam: Eastern Turkeys in the Swamp | "
"7-Day Slam: Eastern Turkeys in the Swamp | "
"7-Day Slam: Eastern Turkeys in the Swamp | "

Video.summary: Phillip Culpepper gets some trigger time in Maryland. He hopes to tag his first Maryland longbeard. Culpepper also calls in a hen and she puts on a show. Follow along as the action unfolds.
Video.title: "A Mad Yelping Hen | Culpepper's First Maryland Gobbler"

Image.alt: "A Mad Yelping Hen | Culpepper's First Maryland Gobbler"
"A Mad Yelping Hen | Culpepper's First Maryland Gobbler"
"A Mad Yelping Hen | Culpepper's First Maryland Gobbler"
